baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 68/100 reflecting strong financial efficiency and direct community impact, though its overall score is tempered by moderate working capital reserves.

Limited third-party impact data available to verify specific long-term outcomes of youth programs.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high donor fit for the Muslim community by maintaining Zakat eligibility and focusing on urgent religious and cultural needs [2][6]. Its mission aligns with core Islamic principles of liberty and justice, providing a clear track record of community engagement [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: MAS LA demonstrates high financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 77.3% and a total revenue of $1,168,118 [1][3]. Its programs, including Tarbiya and Youth Ministry, utilize structured curricula to foster instructional experience and spiritual growth among participants[5].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim American Society of Greater LA empowers the community through spiritual development, youth ministry, and leadership training rooted in Islamic values.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 77.3%, indicating a strong focus on direct service delivery.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.02 to raise every $1.
Verified Zakat-eligible status with clear policies on asnaf categories.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Society of Greater LA (MAS LA) works to move individuals toward God-consciousness and justice through comprehensive religious and social programming [3]. As a Zakat-eligible organization, it facilitates charitable giving to support its mission of conveying Islam with clarity and developing community leaders [2][6].

alignment.explanation: Strong alignment for Muslim donors due to <cite id="7">verified Zakat eligibility</cite> and a mission focused on <cite id="1">God-consciousness and Islamic clarity</cite>.

credibility.explanation: MAS LA is a well-established institution with a <cite id="2">30-year track record</cite> and <cite id="14">77.3% independent board governance</cite>. Financial transparency is maintained through <cite id="4">publicly available Form 990 filings</cite>.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be further enhanced by increasing the <cite id="17">board size beyond 4 members</cite> to meet standard governance benchmarks.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support long-term Islamic identity and leadership development in American Muslim youth through a highly efficient, Zakat-eligible local institution.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill Zakat via the fisabilillah category
Prioritize low overhead and high program-to-expense ratios
Value established community organizations with decades of local history
Believe in the importance of spiritual Tarbiya for the next generation
giving_considerations: Donations support a growing organization with a 120.8% revenue CAGR
Focus is on domestic community building rather than international relief
Governance is currently managed by a small, dedicated board
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking large-scale international humanitarian aid or those who require external third-party RCT evaluations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified program delivery and experienced instruction, though a formal theory of change is still being documented.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MAS LA creates impact by providing spiritual mentorship and leadership training to youth, aiming to produce God-conscious citizens who contribute to social justice.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that spiritual and leadership curricula effectively translate into long-term community resilience.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a 120.8% growth rate, the organization is rapidly scaling its community services and can absorb additional capital for program expansion.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="5">77.3% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring all resources are utilized for community services.
MAS LA demonstrates remarkable fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="12">$0.03 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
Founded in <cite id="2">1993</cite>, the organization brings over 30 years of institutional knowledge to its <cite id="3">youth and leadership programs</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: MAS LA's financial health is characterized by rapid growth and low overhead. The organization saw its revenue climb from <cite id="13">$170,882 in 2021 to $832,882 in 2023</cite>, representing a <cite id="9">120.8% compound annual growth rate</cite>. This growth is supported by a <cite id="14">77.3% independent board</cite>, which provides oversight for its diverse programming. Their impact is quantified by a <cite id="15">cost per beneficiary of approximately $27.22</cite>, which is considered efficient for high-touch religious and leadership development services. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="16">3.6 months of working capital</cite>, providing a stable buffer for its ongoing operations.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Society of Greater LA (MAS LA) is a faith-based nonprofit dedicated to <cite id="1">moving people to strive for God-consciousness, liberty, and justice</cite>. Established in <cite id="2">1993</cite>, the organization has spent over three decades developing a robust infrastructure for <cite id="3">youth ministry, leadership training, and community mobilization</cite> within the Southern California region. MAS LA operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="4">$832,882 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year, with a <cite id="5">77.3% program expense ratio</cite> according to its latest filings. This lean operational model ensures that donor contributions are directed toward core initiatives such as <cite id="6">Tarbiya (spiritual development)</cite> and social justice advocacy. The organization is <cite id="7">verified as Zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the <cite id="8">fisabilillah (in the cause of Allah) category</cite> to support its mission of conveying Islam with clarity. With a <cite id="9">3-year revenue CAGR of 120.8%</cite>, MAS LA has demonstrated significant recent growth, expanding its capacity to serve the local Muslim community through <cite id="10">direct service delivery</cite> and educational curriculum led by instructors with <cite id="11">20-30 years of experience</cite>.
